content.full_text:
July 29, 1980

Roger Canfield  
Senate Minority Caucus  
1116 9th Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Canfield:

This is to confirm the advice previously given to you by Gina Siciliano-Kutchins. You asked a number of questions concerning the filing obligations in connection with the recent special election.

Your first question concerned where incumbent candidates and non-candidates must file late contribution reports. Government Code Section 84214 provides that each candidate or committee who makes a late contribution shall report the late contribution to each office with which that candidate is required to file a campaign statement. Section 81005(b) specifies where the reports for "candidates for and persons holding the office of... Member of the State Legislature..." According to that section such reports must be filed with the "Secretary of State and the clerk of the largest county by population which in whole or in part is included in the election district which the officeholder represents...and two copies with the clerk of the county in which the candidate or officeholder resides..."

Your second question concerns whether the post-election statement required to be filed for the Special Election can be combined with either the post-election primary statement (in the case of candidates) or the semi-annual statement (in the case of non-candidates). The answer to both questions is yes so long as there was no additional activity to be reported after the June 30 closing date for the semi-annual and primary post election statements. If you do combine these statements, you should

Mr. Roger Canfield  
July 29, 1980  
Page Two

indicate on the statement that it is a combined statement and what statements are being combined in it.

Your final question concerns the cumulation period for special election activity for both incumbent and non-incumbent candidates. The cumulation period is the same for both; it will be from either the first reportable transaction (if there was no previous activity) or from the day after the closing date of the last post election statement filed.

I trust this answers your questions. If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Thomas M. Sobel  
Chief  
Legal Division

TMS:nrw
